title: Pakistan and Afghanistan escalate conflict, with unpredictable consequences
subhed: Islamabad launched airstrikes on Kabul, Kandahar, and southeastern Afghanistan on Friday in retaliation for an attack the previous day against Pakistani military bases. Pakistan has accused Afghanistan of harboring Pakistani Taliban responsible for numerous attacks on its territory.

With an estimated 500,000 killed, the Russia-Ukraine war is Europe's deadliest since 1945
subhed: Despite the military secrecy surrounding casualties on both sides and the difficulty of counting civilian victims, efforts outside the battlefield and cross-checking of public data have made it possible to estimate the staggering human cost of four years of war.
byline: By Emmanuel Grynszpan, Nikita Mouravieff, Raphaëlle Aubert (data vizualization) and Manon Romain (data analysis)

title: UK Greens win closely-watched Manchester area election, Labour third
subhed: Labour's disappointing finish comes as Keir Starmer's party hits record levels of unpopularity.

title: 'Jeffrey Epstein's trajectory shows that bluff and deceit remain commonplace'
subhed: Power holders cultivate a closed circle that shields them and reinforces a sense of power, seemingly legitimizing their abuses, sociologists François Denord, Paul Lagneau-Ymonet and Sylvain Thine.

title: Could Punch the monkey make us a little more human?
subhed: Social media users around the world are captivated by the plight of a baby monkey abandoned by its mother and taken in by a Japanese zoo. The story reveals how our tendency toward anthropomorphism can spark a rare, collective empathy.

How Paris is turning into 'Frollywood,' a refuge for American actors
subhed: Whether George Clooney, Matt Dillon or Kristen Stewart, France is attracting more and more actors from across the Atlantic. This trend speaks to both the Trump-era crisis and the decline of the Hollywood model.

title: 'The unnatural alliance between Trump and White evangelicals has cost them dearly, even if it allowed them to gain positions of power'
subhed: Historian Sébastien Fath discusses the history and dynamics of evangelical Protestant churches in an interview with Le Monde. Despite their current vibrancy, their growth is not inevitable, the researcher argues.

title: Four years of war in Ukraine: Five photographers on assignment for Le Monde share their most striking images from 2025
subhed: Five photographers who covered the conflict in 2025 have each chosen an emblematic image of the conflict. Beyond the official figures and statements, these unique perspectives reveal the mark that this new year of war has left on them.
